serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Beef Maggi Mee is approximately 450 g, comprising instant noodles, beef slices, broth, vegetables, and an egg.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food (Foodo Database)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Beef Maggi Mee in the HPB Foodo database. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore data for instant noodle dishes with beef, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked beef and instant noodles. Input values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were retained where consistent with HPB/USDA reference ranges and adjusted for plausibility. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and water data are not available in the primary source for this dish preparation and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beef Maggi Mee. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant noodles (wheat-based, boiled), which reports GI values in the range 52–65. The upper bound of 65 is applied given the refined wheat base of Maggi instant noodles and the high-sodium seasoning packet, which does not lower GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (59.99 g from 450 g × 13.33 g/100g) ÷ 100 = 39.0, rounded to 38.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 400 mg per 100 g; a 450 g serving delivers approximately 1800 mg sodium, representing 78–90% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a bowl, request reduced soy sauce and oyster sauce, and ask for less of the Maggi seasoning packet to keep single-meal sodium below 600 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat-based instant noodles with a GI of approximately 65 and a glycemic load of ~39 per 450 g serving; dietary fibre is only 0.89 g per 100 g, providing minimal glucose-buffering effect.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce the noodle portion to 150–200 g cooked, increase the proportion of vegetables and beef to lower the meal glycemic load, and consider substituting with lower-GI wholemeal or konjac noodles where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.33 g per 100 g (15.0 g per 450 g serving), trans fat 0.11 g per 100 g, cholesterol 26.67 mg per 100 g, and sodium 400 mg per 100 g collectively elevate LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption, request leaner beef cuts, reduce sauce quantities to lower sodium and saturated fat, and increase vegetable content to improve the overall nutrient profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beef is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid), which promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production; sodium at 400 mg per 100 g may exacerbate tissue swelling associated with active RA.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should consider replacing beef with chicken breast or tofu to reduce arachidonic acid load, omit chilli if nightshade sensitivity is present, and reduce soy sauce and oyster sauce to lower sodium-driven inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Maggi Mee is a popular Singaporean hawker-style and home-cooked noodle dish built around Maggi brand instant wheat noodles, tender sliced beef, and a savoury broth seasoned with soy sauce, oyster sauce, and the Maggi seasoning packet. The dish is typically prepared by boiling the instant noodles until just cooked, then combining them with stir-fried or simmered beef slices, leafy green vegetables such as bok choy or choy sum, and a soft-boiled or poached egg. The broth is rich, salty, and umami-forward, with the Maggi seasoning packet contributing a distinctive flavour profile. Texturally, the noodles are soft and slightly springy, the beef is tender, and the vegetables provide a mild crunch. Nutritionally, a 450 g bowl delivers approximately 650 kcal, 30 g protein, 35 g fat, and 60 g carbohydrate, with sodium reaching approximately 1800 mg — a significant proportion of the daily recommended limit. The dish is classified as ultra-processed due to the instant noodle component and is not suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium) based on the refined wheat noodle base, and the glycemic load per serving is approximately 39, placing it in the high glycemic load category. Beef Maggi Mee is widely consumed across Singapore as a quick, affordable, and comforting meal, available at hawker centres, kopitiams, and prepared at home.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Beef Maggi Mee exists in the HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central databases. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore instant noodle dish data and USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked beef and instant noodles. The input values (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were retained where consistent with these reference ranges. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for wheat-based instant noodles. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this specific Beef Maggi Mee preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2024. 2. The input cultural metadata listed 'Filipino' as primary cuisine and included irrelevant ethnic tags ('Coffee', 'Cake'); these have been corrected to 'Singaporean' based on the dish's established origin and consumption context in Singapore. 3. Halal status is set to false as beef sourcing and Maggi seasoning packet halal certification cannot be confirmed without vendor-specific information; consumers should verify halal certification with the specific vendor. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for wheat-based instant noodles (boiled); no direct GI measurement for Beef Maggi Mee was found in approved sources. 5. All core macros (protein_g 6.67, fat_g 7.78, carbohydrate_g 13.33) are non-zero and sourced from adapted HPB/USDA reference data; no zero-macro justification is required.
